page: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores operating at 2.8 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI.

This server type is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 154 GB of local SSD storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-speed disk attachments. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate single-core capability but limited multi-core scaling. The single-core Geekbench score of 1629.0 and stress-ng single-core performance of 1896.69 place it in the average tier. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 3485.0 and Passmark CPU score of 5956.66, are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only large block speed of 29.28 GB/sec and a write large block speed of 25.41 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance also fall into the weaker tier, reflecting the constraints of its 4-vCPU allocation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E4ds_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dense parallel computing. It is best suited for small-to-medium database hosting, caching layers, memory-constrained enterprise applications, and moderate web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. It features 154 GB of local storage and supports premium storage. While single-core performance is average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1629.0, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 3485.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 5956.66. Memory write bandwidth is average at 25.41 GB/sec, but read bandwidth is weaker. Given its high 8.0 GB RAM-per-vCPU ratio and local SSD, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for small-to-medium databases, caching systems, and general memory-optimized enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage. It is optimized for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local SSD storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per-vCPU memory allocation
154 GB of local SSD storage with premium storage capability
Average single-core performance paired with weaker multi-core scaling
Best suited for databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications
